content.full_text:
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Administration 
•• 
Executive/legal 
•• 
Enforcement 
(916) 322·5662 
322·5660 
Darlene Richards-Bloom 
Clerk, Board of Supervisors 
Hall of Records, Room 301 
2281 Tulare Street 
Fresno, CA 
93721-2198 
32~·5901 
322-6441 
September 15, 1983 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322·6444 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-l88 
Dear Ms. Richards-Bloom: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the provlslons of 
the Political Reform Act. 
You stated in your letter that the 
Fresno County Board of Supervisors is designated as the filing 
officer in the conflict of interest codes for each Fresno County 
agency and for each local governmental agency with jurisdiction 
wholly within Fresno County11 
By law, the Board is also the 
code reviewing body with respect to the conflict of interest 
code for these agencies (Government Code Section 82011) .~I 
As the filing officer, the Board of Supervisors receives the 
Statements of Economic Interests filed by each agency's 
designated employees. 
You stated that it has become impossible 
for the Board to monitor personnel changes in the various 
agencies or to ensure filing compliance. 
In order to rectify 
this problem, you have asked the following questions: 
1. 
May the Board of Supervisors, as the code reviewing 
body, delegate its role as the filing officer to the 
agencies covered by codes? 
2. 
If such a delegation occurs, must the Board continue to 
receive the Statements from individuals in high-level 
positions, such as the Superintendent of Schools or the 
managers of the various agencies? 
11 This does not include any city agencies. 
~I Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. 


Darlene Richards-Bloom 
September 15, 1983 
Page 2 
3. 
May the Board implement the change and modify each 
conflict of interest code, or must each agency formally 
adopt the change? 
In response to your first question, although the Board of 
Supervisors is required to be the code reviewing body, it is not 
required to be the filing officer. 
Section 87500(k) specifies 
that the code reviewing body may designate in an agency's code 
that the agency, rather than the code reviewing body, will 
receive the originals of Statements. 
In response to your question concerning the filing of 
Statements by a person holding the position of Superintendent of 
Schools or agency manager, Section 87500(j) provides that the 
original Statement of the head of an agency will be filed with 
his or her agency who will make and retain a copy and forward 
the original to the code reviewing body. 
In its discretion, the 
code reviewing body may decide that it will receive the original 
and that the agency will not retain a copy. 
Currently, the 
Board appears to be using the second filing method. 
By 
switching to the first method, the agency will receive the 
Statement first and it can determine whether the Statement was 
filed in a timely manner. 
As the code reviewing body, the Board of Supervisors has the 
power to amend the agencies' conflict of interest codes in order 
to effectuate the changes described above. 
The language of 
Section 87500(k) states that the role of filing officer shall 
rest with either "the agency or the code reviewing body, ~ 
provided by the code reviewing body in the agency's conflict of 
interest code," and the language of Section 87500(j) states 
that, in the case of the Statements of the heads of agencies, 
the choice between the methods of filing is at the "discretion" 
of the code reviewing body. 
I hope that I have clarified this matter for you. 
If you 
have any additional questions, please feel free to contact me at 
(961) 322-5901. 
Very truly yours, 
t~ha~~~ 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
JSM:plh 


........ "" ... .,. of Supervisors 
Chairman 
Jeff Reich 
5th District 
August IS, 1983 
Ms. Alice Hughes 
Vice Chairman 
A. Vernon Conrad 
4th District 
Betty Ramacher 
Sharon Levy 
3rd District 
2nd District 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Alice: 
Deran KOligian 
1st District 
RE: 
Request for Legal Opinion on Proposal to Change 
Filing Officer Designation 
Some time ago I spoke with you regarding an opinion as 
to whether the Board of Supervisors had the authority to 
designate someone, other than the Clerk of the Board, as 
filing officer in the various County Conflict of Interest 
Codes. 
You asked that I explain the existing situation 
and my proposal. 
Darlene Richards 
Clerk 
In Fresno County each school 
strict, special district, 
etc., has its own conflict of interest code. 
When these 
codes were first adopted, the Clerk of the Board of 
Supervisors was designated as the filing officer. 
This 
has led to problems and confusion in the appropriate 
administration of the act because I, as the filing officer, 
must depend on the various districts to inform me of their 
personnel changes. 
Taking the 37 school districts as an example. 
Each of 
these districts have their own code and their Boards of 
Trustees and other district employees are required to 
file with me. 
However, administratively I have no 
means of monitoring their personnel changes to 
sure 
filing 
e. 
Therefore, it would appear appropr 
that the 
ent of Schools be des 
fil 
ts and 
the 
be requ 
Room 301. Hall of Recordsl2281 Tulare Street/fresno. California 93721-2198/(209) 488-3529 
Equal Employment Opportunity -
Affirmative Action -
Handicap Employer 


Ms. Alice Hughes 
August 15, 1983 
Page 2 
I would appreciate your review and assistance on the 
following issues: 
o 
Is it legal, under Government Code Section 87500, 
to have the County Superintendent of Schools 
designated as filing officer for the school 
districts and other district managers as filing 
officers for their Boards of Directors, with only 
the superintendent/manager filing with the County? 
o 
If so, can the Board of Supervisors, as the code 
reviewing body, initiate this change and modify all 
the conflict of interest codes without each separate 
district having to formally initiate this proposal. 
Please forward your legal opinion to me as soon as possible. 
Thank you for your continued assistance. 
If you have any 
questions, please give me a call 
Sincerely, 
Darlene J. Richards-Bloom, Clerk 
Board of 
visors 
cc: 
John Slutter, Deputy County Counsel 
DJB/ar

sections.question: s, please give me a call
sections.conclusion: In response to your first question, although the Board of Supervisors is required to be the code reviewing body, it is not required to be the filing officer. Section 87500(k) specifies that the code reviewing body may designate in an agency's code that the agency, rather than the code reviewing body, will receive the originals of Statements. In response to your question concerning the filing of Statements by a person holding the position of Superintendent of Schools or agency manager, Section 87500(j) provides that the original Statement of the head of an agency will be filed with his or her agency who will make and retain a copy and forward the original to the code reviewing body. In its discretion, the code reviewing body may decide that it will receive the original and that the agency will not retain a copy. As the code reviewing body, the Board of Supervisors has the power to amend the agencies' conflict of interest codes in order to effectuate the changes described above.
sections.question_synthetic: May the Fresno County Board of Supervisors, as the code reviewing body, delegate its filing officer role to individual agencies, continue to receive statements from high-level officials under a delegated system, and unilaterally modify conflict of interest codes to implement this change without each agency's formal adoption?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Board of Supervisors may delegate its filing officer role to agencies under Government Code Section 87500(k), may choose to receive only statements from high-level agency heads while agencies retain copies under Section 87500(j), and has the authority as the code reviewing body to unilaterally amend the conflict of interest codes to implement these changes without requiring formal adoption by each agency.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Complete advice letter with clear Q&C sections. Document includes both the incoming request letter (dated August 15, 1983) and the FPPC's response letter (dated September 15, 1983). All three questions and responses are present. Minor OCR artifact noted in source document but did not impair extraction.

QUESTION: s, please give me a call

CONCLUSION: In response to your first question, although the Board of Supervisors is required to be the code reviewing body, it is not required to be the filing officer. Section 87500(k) specifies that the code reviewing body may designate in an agency's code that the agency, rather than the code reviewing body, will receive the originals of Statements. In response to your question concerning the filing of Statements by a person holding the position of Superintendent of Schools or agency manager, Section 87500(j) provides that the original Statement of the head of an agency will be filed with his or her agency who will make and retain a copy and forward the original to the code reviewing body. In its discretion, the code reviewing body may decide that it will receive the original and that the agency will not retain a copy. As the code reviewing body, the Board of Supervisors has the power to amend the agencies' conflict of interest codes in order to effectuate the changes described above.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that the Fresno County Board of Supervisors may delegate its filing officer duties to individual county agencies and modify conflict of interest codes accordingly, while retaining discretion over whether to receive original statements from agency heads. This resolves the Board's administrative burden in monitoring personnel changes across multiple districts and agencies.
